title: Alone
content:
A very old woman
 Lives in yon house - 
 The squeak of the cricket,
 The stir of the mouse,
 Are all she knows
 Of the earth and us.

 Once she was young,
 Would dance and play,
 Like many another
 Young popinjay;
 And run to her mother
 At dusk of day.

 And colours bright
 She delighted in;
 The fiddle to hear,
 And to lift her chin,
 And sing as small
 As a twittering wren.

 But age apace
 Comes at last to all;
 And a lone house filled
 With the cricket's call;
 And the scampering mouse
 In the hollow wall.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
